name: Bachelor of Medicine & Surgery (MBChB)
career: Becomes a medical doctor (MD). Works in hospitals, clinics, public health, surgery, or specialises into fields like paediatrics, obstetrics, oncology. High demand across East Africa and globally. Can pursue postgraduate specialisation via MMed.

name: Bachelor of Pharmacy
career: Pharmacist in hospitals, community pharmacies, pharmaceutical industry, or drug regulatory bodies (NDA Uganda). Can specialise in clinical pharmacy, industrial pharmacy, or pharmaceutical research.

name: Bachelor of Dental Surgery
career: Dental surgeon in private practice, public hospitals, or specialised dental clinics. Can specialise in orthodontics, oral surgery, or paediatric dentistry.

name: Bachelor of Science in Nursing
career: Registered nurse in hospitals, community health centres, NGOs, or as a nurse educator. Opportunities in Uganda, Middle East, UK, and other international placements.

name: Bachelor of Biomedical Laboratory Technology
career: Medical laboratory scientist in hospitals, diagnostic labs, research institutions, and public health agencies. Growing demand in diagnostics and disease surveillance.

name: Bachelor of Veterinary Medicine
career: Veterinarian in livestock, wildlife conservation, food safety, government veterinary services, or animal research. Opportunities with NGOs and international animal health organisations.

name: Bachelor of Agriculture
career: Agricultural officer, farm manager, agribusiness entrepreneur, researcher, or development worker with NGOs and government extension services.

name: Bachelor of Engineering (Civil)
career: Civil engineer designing roads, bridges, buildings, dams, and water systems. Works with government (UNRA, NWSC), construction firms, and consultancies. High demand in Uganda's infrastructure growth.

name: Bachelor of Engineering (Electrical)
career: Electrical engineer in power generation, telecommunications, electronics design, or automation. Works with UMEME, MTN, Airtel, and engineering consultancies.

name: Bachelor of Engineering (Mechanical)
career: Mechanical engineer in manufacturing, oil & gas, automotive, or HVAC sectors. Opportunities in Uganda's growing industrial and energy sectors.

name: Bachelor of Architecture
career: Architect designing residential, commercial, and public buildings. Works in private firms, government (MoWT), or runs own practice. Urban planning opportunities.

name: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
career: Software developer, data scientist, systems analyst, cybersecurity specialist, or AI/ML engineer. One of the fastest-growing fields in Uganda and globally. High earning potential.

name: Bachelor of Science in Software Engineering
career: Software engineer building web apps, mobile apps, enterprise systems, or embedded systems. Strong demand locally and remote work opportunities globally.

name: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
career: IT support specialist, systems administrator, network engineer, or IT project manager. Broad demand across private sector, NGOs, and government.

name: Bachelor of Statistics
career: Statistician, data analyst, or actuary in banking, insurance, research, government statistics bureaus (UBOS), or international organisations (UN, World Bank).

name: Bachelor of Commerce
career: Accountant, finance officer, banker, auditor, or business analyst. Eligible to pursue CPA, ACCA, or CFA professional certifications afterward.

name: Bachelor of Business Administration
career: Business manager, entrepreneur, HR manager, marketing professional, or operations manager across private sector and government agencies.

name: Bachelor of Economics
career: Economist in government (MoFPED, BoU), international organisations (IMF, World Bank), banks, research institutions, or NGOs. Strong analytical career.

name: Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
career: Advocate, state attorney, corporate lawyer, legal counsel, judge (after experience), or human rights lawyer. Bar Course (Law Development Centre) required after graduation to practise.

name: Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
career: Same career pathways as other LLB programmes. UCU has a strong moot court culture and produces many advocates and human rights practitioners.

name: Bachelor of Mass Communication
career: Journalist, media producer, public relations officer, communications manager, or digital content creator. Growing demand in Uganda's media and NGO sectors.

name: Bachelor of Education (Science)
career: Secondary school teacher in science subjects. Can progress to head of department, deputy principal, or principal. Government teachers are on the public service pay scale.

name: Bachelor of Social Work & Social Administration
career: Social worker, community development officer, probation officer, NGO programme manager, or humanitarian worker. Strong demand in Uganda's large NGO sector.

name: Bachelor of Science in Quantity Surveying
career: Quantity surveyor estimating and managing construction costs. Works with construction companies, government (MoWT), and consulting firms. Growing demand with Uganda's construction boom.

name: Bachelor of Medicine & Surgery (MBChB)
career: Medical doctor in Kenya's public and private hospitals. Can pursue postgraduate specialisation. Strong demand across East Africa and diaspora.

name: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
career: Software engineer, data scientist, or systems architect. Kenya is East Africa's tech hub (Silicon Savannah) — excellent local and international job market.

name: Bachelor of Engineering (Civil)
university: Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture & Technology
career: Civil engineer for infrastructure development across Kenya and the region. Kenya's massive SGR and road projects drive strong demand.

name: Bachelor of Commerce (Accounting)
career: Accountant, auditor, or finance professional in Kenya's large formal sector. CPA-K certification pathway available after graduation.

name: Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
career: Advocate of the High Court of Kenya. Corporate lawyer, judge, academic, or human rights practitioner. Kenya School of Law admission required after graduation.

name: Bachelor of Pharmacy
career: Pharmacist in hospitals, community pharmacies, or pharmaceutical manufacturing. Kenya's growing healthcare sector needs more pharmacists.

name: Bachelor of Education (Science)
career: Science teacher in secondary schools across Kenya's expanding public education system. TSC employment pathway with structured career progression.

name: Bachelor of Science in Nursing
university: Kenya Medical Training College / Moi University
career: Registered nurse eligible for practice in Kenya's public and private hospitals. High international demand — pathway to UK, Middle East, and Canada placements.

name: Bachelor of Economics
career: Economist in Kenya's central bank (CBK), Treasury, World Bank, IMF, or private sector. Strong analytical career with excellent regional demand.

name: Bachelor of Engineering (Electrical)
university: Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture & Technology
career: Electrical engineer in Kenya's power sector (KPLC, KenGen), telecoms (Safaricom, Airtel), or engineering consultancies. Strong demand from renewable energy projects.

name: Bachelor of Medicine & Surgery (MD)
university: Muhimbili University of Health & Allied Sciences
career: Medical doctor in Tanzania's public health system (MoHCDGEC) or private hospitals. MUHAS is the premier medical university in Tanzania. Can pursue postgraduate specialisation (MMed).

name: Bachelor of Pharmacy
university: Muhimbili University of Health & Allied Sciences
career: Pharmacist in Tanzania's hospitals, community pharmacies, or the pharmaceutical manufacturing sector. TFDA registration required to practise.

name: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science & Engineering
career: Software engineer, data scientist, systems analyst, or IT manager. Tanzania's growing fintech and mobile money sector (M-Pesa ecosystem) creates strong local demand.

name: Bachelor of Engineering (Civil & Water Resources)
career: Civil engineer for Tanzania's TANZAM highway, SGR railway, and large dam projects. Government (TANROADS, RELI ASSETS) and private sector opportunities.

name: Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
career: Advocate of the High Court of Tanzania. Corporate lawyer, state attorney, or legal academic. Law School of Tanzania (LST) Bar examinations required to practise.

name: Bachelor of Commerce (Accounting & Finance)
career: Accountant, banker, or finance officer in Tanzania's formal economy. NBAA certification pathway available. Strong demand in the banking and donor-funded project sector.

name: Bachelor of Science in Nursing
university: Muhimbili University of Health & Allied Sciences
career: Registered nurse in Tanzania's public and mission hospitals. Growing international placement opportunities in the Middle East and Europe. Can progress to specialised nursing or nurse tutoring.

name: Bachelor of Education (Science)
career: Science teacher in Tanzania's secondary schools. TSC/Teachers Service Department employment pathway. Chronic shortage of qualified science teachers means strong job security.

name: Bachelor of Engineering (Electrical)
university: Ardhi University / University of Dar es Salaam
career: Electrical engineer in TANESCO, telecoms (Vodacom, Airtel TZ), or engineering consultancies. Tanzania's rural electrification programme drives consistent demand.

name: Bachelor of Agriculture & Food Security
career: Agronomist, food security officer, or agribusiness professional. Sokoine is Tanzania's top agriculture university. Strong demand from government extension services, FAO, and World Food Programme.

name: Bachelor of Environmental Science & Management
career: Environmental officer, conservation scientist, or sustainability consultant. Tanzania's massive wildlife and conservation sector (TANAPA, WWF, WCS) creates strong demand.

name: Bachelor of Tourism & Hospitality Management
university: Institute of Finance Management / Ardhi University
career: Hotel manager, tour operator, or hospitality professional in Tanzania's massive tourism sector — the country's largest foreign exchange earner (Serengeti, Zanzibar, Kilimanjaro).
